title: Period End Close Payment Cutoff
description: A reconciliation and back-office workflow that resolves breaks, validates accounting alignment, and closes audit-critical exceptions. Focus case: period end close payment cutoff.
assumptions: Source systems deliver payment batches and confirmations with stable identifiers usable for automated linking.
Cutoff timestamp and period definitions are authoritative and synchronized across processing nodes.
Operational roles (resolvers, approvers) are available and have the required authority to change holds and approve closures.
Supporting documents and external confirmations can be attached to cases and retained for audit.
risks: Late or missing confirmations from external counterparties resulting in unresolved cases at close.
Incorrect mapping between payment batches and ledger accounts causing misstatements.
System time skew or differing cutoff definitions across systems leading to inclusion/exclusion errors.
Excessive manual overrides bypassing control flags and degrading auditability.
